mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the subsequent 5 trading days as investors price in lower future consumption.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and consumer credit indicates broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon when both series rise concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both proxies rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its historical trend while capacity utilization remains elevated, supply-side constraints compress profit margins and force equity valuations lower, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations where industrial production peaks above 110, refuting the margin compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands aggregate corporate earnings potential, which directly supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as market participants price in stronger macroeconomic fundamentals.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if quarterly GDP revisions consistently show negative correlation with 5-day forward equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds the Federal Reserve's 2 percent target, the central bank is forced to maintain restrictive policy, which elevates the equity risk premium and suppresses forward S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where inflation breaches the 2.5 percent threshold, indicating the market does not price in policy tightening costs.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth and a falling unemployment rate signals a labor market that is overheating, prompting fears of imminent wage-push inflation and triggering a negative S&P 500 forward return over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a positive Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline when payrolls rise and unemployment falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase where real GDP growth accelerates quarter-over-quarter while unemployment remains below 4 percent, equity markets experience a sustained risk-on rally, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if forward returns turn negative in more than 40% of post-shock recovery episodes where GDP accelerates and unemployment stays below 4 percent.
